calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-04-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for and with Debra Dolch (Administrator). Chet Allen (Beneficiary) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Allen raised the issue of missing items, stated that he did not agree with the appraisals and spoke of the conflict he has with regard to Attorney Seth Skootksy's excessive fees. Ms. Rasch stated that they turned over everything that was in their possession to Chat Allen and that they never received any objections to the Inventory and Appraisal of the Probate Referee. Ms. Rasch also stated that there are no signed receipts from Mr. Allen for the preliminary distributions, and asked the Court to order Mr. Allen to sign them or to proceed without them. The Court stated that it will not order Mr. Allen to sign the receipts. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-28-2013 Continued To Feb-27-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Skootsky Attorney of record for Chet Allen. Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch (Administrator). Chet Allen in Propria Persona.

Mr. Allen confirmed that he received half of his Father's property, stated that he had several complaints about the mediation and his Attorney's fees, and that he received two checks ($196,115.89 from the 2nd preliminary distribution which was sent to Mr. Skootksy's office and $97,700.00 as ordered in the first preliminary distribution). In addition, Mr. Allen stated that he received the vehicle, that the whole estate was not inventoried and that he was not objecting to the Administrator's fees or her Attorney's fees. The Court explained to Mr. Allen that the mediation fees and his Attorney's fees were not before the Court today, that she could not advise him as to how to get them before her, that the objections need to be filed and stated that personal property is the only remaining issue. The accounting petition was continued to February, 27 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the objections and for filing of a Substitution of Attorney. Objections are to be filed by February 13, 2013.

Judge: Lillian Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-14-2013 Continued To Jan-28-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1566 Newcomb Street S.F.)
Off Calendar Aug-01-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for and with Debra Dolch.

The sale was confirmed at $285,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review/receipt of the order). No additional bond is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy C. Garcia.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1109-1111 Phelps St. S.F.)
Off Calendar Jun-25-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch.

The sale was confirmed at $300,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). No additional bond is required.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Loos.

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement.
Off Calendar Sep-16-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch. Jerry Nastari for Ann Marie Allen. Seth Skootksy for Chet Allen.

The Petition for Approval of the Settlement Agreement amd the Petition for Letters of Administration were granted (subject to receipt/review of the orders). The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was taken off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-23-2011 Off Calendar

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement.
Off Calendar Jul-19-2011 Continued To Aug-23-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch (Special Administrator). Seth Skootsky for Chet Allen. Jerry Nastari for Ann Marie Allen.

The Court stated that the Petition for Approval of the Settlement Agreement seems appropriate for approval but that it was unclear as to what the Parties intentions are with regard to the $35,000.00 to the specific Devisees, that the will should be admitted subject to distribution pursuant to the terms of the settlement or that they should proceed intestate. Counsel agreed to continue to matter for three weeks to resolve the issues and to cure the notice issues. Mr. Skootsky will address the issue of the four Devisees with unknown addresses. All matters were continued to August 23, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR A Status Conference
Off Calendar Mar-30-2011 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for Chet Allen.

Mr. Der requested a continuance stating that mediation has not been completed, that a new Attorney just substituted in for Mr. Jellins and that mediation was scheduled for the end of April. The matter was continued to May 25, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that there was no proof of publication for Chet Allen and that the notice of the contest needs to be served.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-09-2011 Continued To Mar-30-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Skootsky for and with Chet Allen. Nicholas Jellins for and with Anne Marie Allen. Nancy Rasch for and with Debra Dolch. Other Family members also present.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Ms. Dolch continuing to serve as Special Administrator or Anne Marie Allen being appointed. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than March 30, 2011. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by March 25, 2010. A status conference is set for March 30, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petitions were also continued to March 30, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Dolch's powers as Special Adminstrator with general powers are extended until further order of the Court (Ms. Rasch will submit a corrected order). Mr. Jellins is to provide information regarding the automobiles to Ms. Dolch for appraisal purposes.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
